caption: For the thoughts that get louder after midnight. #emotionalvocabulary #selfawareness #verbsy
title: words for overthinking at night

words.definition: repeatedly thinking about the same worry or regret.
words.example: When your mind reopens the same tab all night.

words.definition: a restless feeling of worry or unease.
words.example: Calm on the outside. Static underneath.

words.definition: to imagine the worst possible outcome.
words.example: A small problem becomes a whole disaster movie.

caption: Social intelligence starts with noticing the pattern. #socialintelligence #communication #verbsy
title: words for reading the room

words.definition: skill at saying the right thing without causing offense.
words.example: Truth, but with emotional timing.

words.definition: careful to consider risks before speaking or acting.
words.example: You can feel the stakes before you move.

words.definition: a warm, easy connection between people.
words.example: When the conversation stops feeling like effort.

caption: Tired is too small for some kinds of exhaustion. #wordchoice #emotionalvocabulary #verbsy
title: stop saying very tired

words.definition: drained of energy or vitality.
words.example: Not sleepy. Spiritually low-battery.

words.definition: slow, weak, or lacking energy in a graceful way.
words.example: Tired, but almost poetic about it.

words.definition: completely used up emotionally or physically.
words.example: When there is nothing left to perform with.

caption: Precision is attractive when it has taste. #mindsetwords #communication #verbsy
title: words for high standards

words.definition: very attentive to detail and hard to please.
words.example: They notice what most people miss.

words.definition: able to judge quality with careful taste.
words.example: Selective because the difference is obvious to them.

words.definition: requiring great care, precision, or effort.
words.example: Their standards are not casual.

caption: The aftermath has its own vocabulary. #communication #selfawareness #verbsy
title: feelings after a hard conversation

words.definition: emotional release after expressing something difficult.
words.example: The relief after finally saying it.

words.definition: deep regret for something you did wrong.
words.example: An apology beginning inside the body.

words.definition: a sense that something painful has been resolved.
words.example: Not fixed, but no longer unfinished.

words.definition: polished, confident, and socially graceful.
words.example: Smooth without trying to look smooth.

words.definition: elegant, tasteful, and free from roughness.
words.example: Simple, but clearly chosen.

words.definition: relating to clothes or personal style.
words.example: A sartorial detail can change the whole room.

words.definition: charming, smooth, and confident.
words.example: Charming, but controlled.

words.definition: worldly, cultured, and at ease in many places.
words.example: At home in more than one world.

caption: A little delusion deserves better vocabulary. #mindsetwords #vocabulary #verbsy
title: words for the main character delusion

words.definition: using language that sounds overly grand or important.
words.example: When the group chat becomes a speech.

words.definition: excessively proud of yourself or your achievements.
words.example: Confidence with a mirror in its hand.

words.definition: romantically idealistic in an impractical way.
words.example: A beautiful plan with no logistics.

caption: Some depth is just fog with confidence. #psychologywords #communication #verbsy
title: words for fake deep

words.definition: seeming true or wise, but actually misleading.
words.example: Sounds profound until you touch it.

words.definition: trying too hard to sound morally wise.
words.example: A quote-post personality in one word.

words.definition: full of dull, overused statements.
words.example: All lesson, no insight.

caption: The internet has a mood, and it is not always healthy. #culturewords #selfawareness #verbsy
title: words for being too online

words.definition: opposing change out of fear or resentment.
words.example: A whole opinion built from a flinch.

words.definition: done mainly to be seen, not sincerely felt.
words.example: A value performed for applause.

words.definition: strongly argumentative or attacking.
words.example: Every thought arrives ready to fight.

caption: Not every kind of detachment feels the same. #emotionalvocabulary #wordchoice #verbsy
title: words for not caring anymore

words.definition: showing little interest, concern, or emotion.
words.example: When even reacting feels like effort.

words.definition: no longer believing in something you once admired.
words.example: The magic left, and you noticed.

words.definition: unimpressed because you have seen too much before.
words.example: Bored, but with experience behind it.
